summary: Documentation-only PR adding docs/standards.md, a decomp PR and worker-output review checklist. It codified expectations for natural C source recovery, matching tactics, data and naming discipline, truthful headers/includes, reviewable formatting, and verification with local builds plus objdiff/checkdiff evidence. The PR was closed without merge, so these standards should be treated as proposed guidance from this slice rather than adopted repository policy.

key_files.notes: Introduced Melee PR Review QA Standards covering source rules, matching tactic rules, data/naming/file rules, and verification rules.

what_changed: Added a new docs/standards.md review checklist for decomp PRs and worker output.
Defined source-style rules encouraging maintainable original-looking C over generated-looking matching artifacts.

Documented preference order for data access: real struct fields, correct GroundVars/FighterVars/ItemVars union arms, temporary padded structs, M2C_FIELD, then raw pointer arithmetic only as a last resort.
Added guidance for recognizing header inlines, especially HSD_JObj helpers and jobj.h assert line numbers.
Specified project assert/report macro usage, including HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, and OSPanic.
Warned against inventing fake assert strings, report globals, dummy storage, static literals, or data anchors solely to force matches.

Outlined matching tactic safeguards for PAD_STACK, FORCE_PAD_STACK, declaration order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy stack locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs.
Added rules against casually introducing new pragmas, register steering, dont_inline pragmas, global_optimizer off, or inline assembly outside SDK-like cases.
Codified data section and TU split review rules for .sdata, .sdata2, .sbss, .rodata, .data, symbols.txt metadata, local @NNN labels, assert filenames, string clusters, object names, and float groups.
Added naming rules: use semantic names only when evidenced; otherwise keep fn_<addr>, lbl_<addr>, or module-address names; prefer clear short role locals like fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj.

Added header/include rules requiring truthful prototypes, removal of stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, declarations in owning headers, established include style, and configure.py --require-protos when applicable.

Added verification expectations: narrow touched-object builds, full ninja for wider blast radius, objdiff/checkdiff for claimed matches, adjacent-function inspection for risky changes, and explicit regression reporting.
smart_moves: The document separates natural source recovery from match-only tactics, making it easier for reviewers to reject code that matches but preserves generated or fake structure.
It treats M2C_FIELD and raw pointer math as temporary bridges rather than acceptable final source style, which encourages real type recovery.
It explicitly ties risky matching tactics to required local evidence, reducing the chance of cargo-culted PAD_STACK, pragma, dummy-local, or lifetime hacks.
The guidance distinguishes data ownership evidence from generated-address artifacts, discouraging fake named symbols and dummy storage.
The checklist calls out adjacent-function effects from pragmas, includes, data, literals, and TU ownership changes, which is important because matching fixes can regress neighboring code.
It encodes conservative naming rules that balance searchability and honesty: semantic names when supported, address-style names when not.
It emphasizes truthful headers and prototype recovery, including requiring real owning headers instead of source-local declarations that hide missing prototypes.
decomp_lessons: Prefer maintainable original-looking C first; keep unrolled or goto-heavy generated code only after cleaner loop or structured forms were tested and failed.
When repeated blocks differ only by an index, pointer, or counter, try for/while/do-while forms and exact int counters for MWCC loop-unrolling behavior.
Do not mutate the only copy of a base pointer if later code still needs the original address; index from the original base where needed.
Recover real struct fields and correct union arms before resorting to M2C_FIELD or raw offset arithmetic.
Use header inline helpers when identifiable from assert file and line evidence; do not manually expand inlines just to match unless objdiff evidence supports it.
Use existing project macros for asserts, reports, and common expressions when they represent the source-level operation.
Keep literals inline unless symbol metadata, section placement, or surrounding source proves named data ownership.
Treat data section placement and TU split evidence as matching-relevant, not cosmetic.
Separate broad formatting or mechanical rename sweeps from semantic matching work when possible.
Every claimed match should be backed by local build plus objdiff/checkdiff evidence, not just a broad builds-locally claim.
naming_conventions: Use semantic names only when the role is evidenced.
Keep fn_<addr>, lbl_<addr>, or module-address names when semantics are not proven.
Use short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj when those roles are clear.
Clean up generated temp_rXX-style names once the role is known.
Do not invent semantic names from guesses or rename address-style symbols before source meaning is supported.
Use local @NNN labels for compiler-local data when appropriate.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Require targeted evidence before keeping PAD_STACK or FORCE_PAD_STACK.

Require evidence for declaration-order changes, branch-local or widened local lifetimes, dummy stack locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global or extern access instead of a struct path, and temporary structs with offset padding.
Do not keep a matching tactic if it shifts nearby functions, changes data order, or hides a real type.
Scope unavoidable pragmas tightly and prove they do not affect adjacent functions.
Avoid new #pragma global_optimizer off, unexplained #pragma dont_inline, unbalanced pragma push/pop blocks, register steering, and inline assembly unless no reasonable C alternative exists.
Use grouped switches, ternaries, ABS, MIN, MAX, CLAMP, and structured control flow when assembly shape suggests natural source forms.
Run objdiff or checkdiff for every claimed matched symbol or unit and inspect adjacent functions for risky changes.

review_feedback.feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
Validation reported git diff --cached --check; build/tests were not run because the change was documentation-only.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so there is no evidence in this slice that the standards were accepted into master.

searchable_terms: Prefer Typed Fields Over Pointer Math
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged or replaced docs/standards.md.
Search for PR comments citing PAD_STACK or FORCE_PAD_STACK evidence requirements.
Search for decomp PRs where reviewers asked to replace M2C_FIELD or raw pointer math with typed fields.
Search for PRs involving HSD_JObj inline recognition from jobj.h assert line numbers.
Search for standards or agent instructions that reference PR #2607.
